articles.title_original: Intune Custom Compliance Could Move Beyond the 8 Hour Wait
articles.headline: Intune Custom Compliance May Reduce the Eight-Hour Evaluation Delay
articles.summary: Intune custom compliance policies may support faster evaluations instead of making administrators wait up to eight hours for refreshed results. Shorter delays could help security teams detect and remediate noncompliant endpoints sooner.

articles.title_original: CVE-2026-50012 Squid: Memory corruption in cache_digest reply handling
articles.headline: Squid cache digest flaw can corrupt memory
articles.summary: CVE-2026-50012 causes memory corruption while Squid handles cache digest replies. Administrators operating affected proxy or caching infrastructure should prioritize vendor updates because memory corruption can cause crashes or potentially more severe compromise.

articles.title_original: CVE-2026-47729 Squid: Memory disclosure in FTP gateway
articles.headline: Squid FTP gateway flaw can expose memory contents
articles.summary: CVE-2026-47729 allows memory disclosure through Squid's FTP gateway. Administrators using Squid for FTP proxying should evaluate affected versions and apply vendor guidance to reduce the risk of sensitive data exposure.

articles.title_original: CVE-2026-62299 CoreDNS: rewrite-plugin EDNS0 response-revert nil-pointer panic (remote DoS) when a downstream plugin returns a response with no OPT record
articles.headline: CoreDNS rewrite plugin flaw enables remote denial of service
articles.summary: CVE-2026-62299 triggers a nil-pointer panic when the CoreDNS rewrite plugin processes a downstream response without an EDNS0 OPT record. Administrators should review affected deployments because remote requests could disrupt DNS availability.

articles.title_original: CVE-2026-62309 CoreDNS: proxyproto plugin panics on PPv2 datagram with non-UDP transport — single 28-byte packet remote DoS
articles.headline: CoreDNS flaw lets one 28-byte packet trigger remote denial of service
articles.summary: CVE-2026-62309 causes the CoreDNS proxyproto plugin to panic when it receives a crafted Proxy Protocol v2 datagram using a non-UDP transport. Administrators running affected DNS infrastructure should assess exposure and prioritize available updates or mitigations.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15905 Use after free in Aura
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes Chromium use-after-free vulnerability CVE-2026-15905
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has inherited Chromium's fix for a use-after-free vulnerability in the Aura interface framework. Administrators should prioritize Edge updates to reduce the risk of browser-based exploitation.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15904 Use after free in Ozone
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium Ozone
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15904, a use-after-free vulnerability in Ozone. Administrators should deploy the latest Edge security update to reduce browser exploitation risk.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15903 Out of bounds read and write in V8
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes out-of-bounds access flaw in Chromium V8
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15903, an out-of-bounds read and write vulnerability in V8. Administrators should prioritize the latest Edge update because memory corruption in the JavaScript engine can expose users to browser-based attacks.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15902 Use after free in Cast
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium Cast
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15902, a use-after-free vulnerability in Cast functionality. Administrators should apply the latest Edge security update, especially on devices where browser casting is available.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15901 Use after free in Network
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium networking
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15901, a use-after-free vulnerability in network handling. Administrators should update Edge across managed devices to reduce exposure from malicious or compromised web services.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15900 Use after free in GPU
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium GPU code
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15900, a use-after-free vulnerability in GPU processing. Administrators should deploy the latest Edge update to protect endpoints from malicious graphics content that could trigger memory corruption.

articles.title_original: Chromium: CVE-2026-15899 Use after free in CameraCapture
articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium CameraCapture
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15899, a use-after-free vulnerability in CameraCapture. Administrators should update managed browsers to limit exposure when users interact with camera-enabled web content.

articles.title_original: Microsoft Viva: Copilot Analytics - Agent 365 dashboard for managers
articles.headline: Copilot Analytics adds Agent 365 activity dashboard for managers
articles.summary: Managers will gain access to current and historical Agent 365 activity through the Copilot Analytics Agent Dashboard. The August 2026 release will help organizations monitor adoption and understand the use of centrally registered agents.

articles.title_original: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection - Auto-labeling scale increase for SharePoint and OneDrive
articles.headline: Purview raises daily auto-labeling capacity to 500,000 files
articles.summary: Microsoft Purview will increase auto-labeling capacity for SharePoint and OneDrive from 100,000 to 500,000 files per tenant each day. Administrators can protect existing data faster, reduce sensitivity-labeling gaps, and better prepare content for Microsoft 365 Copilot.

articles.title_original: Outlook: All Day events to show up more clearly in the Mail module Calendar Preview
articles.headline: Outlook highlights all-day scheduling conflicts in meeting invitations
articles.summary: Outlook on the web and Windows will make conflicts with out-of-office periods and busy all-day events easier to identify. The change may reduce scheduling mistakes but has limited relevance to security administration.

articles.title_original: Microsoft Teams: External phone call routing (GCC High, DoD)
articles.headline: Teams adds external call routing for GCC High and DoD users
articles.summary: Teams users in GCC High and DoD environments will be able to route all external calls to voicemail or their unanswered-call settings. Administrators should review calling policies before the September 2026 rollout, although the change has limited security impact.

articles.title_original: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Use Agent 365 agents within Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat and Declarative Agents
articles.headline: Microsoft 365 Copilot connects Agent 365 agents through Agent2Agent
articles.summary: Agents published to Agent 365 will work as connected agents in Agent Builder and Copilot Studio when they implement the Agent2Agent protocol. Administrators can centrally manage reusable agents through the Microsoft 365 admin center ahead of general availability in August 2026.

articles.title_original: Microsoft at Black Hat USA 2026: Defending trust in the age of AI and supply chain attacks
articles.headline: Microsoft Maps New Trust-Based Attacks Across Software, Identity, and AI
articles.summary: Microsoft outlines how attackers exploit trusted packages, build pipelines, identities, tools, and AI agents to reach enterprise systems. IT administrators should review access controls and monitoring across software supply chains, cloud services, and AI workloads.

articles.title_original: CVE-2026-56159 DHCP Server Service Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
articles.headline: Microsoft adds acknowledgements for DHCP Server vulnerability CVE-2026-56159
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the advisory for the DHCP Server remote code execution vulnerability with acknowledgements only. The change adds no new technical details or remediation guidance, so administrators do not need to alter existing patching plans.

articles.title_original: Autopilot Required Apps No Longer Need to Wait 60 Minutes
articles.headline: Intune Fixes the 60-Minute Autopilot Delay for Required Apps
articles.summary: Intune Management Extension 1.103.101.0 appears to remove the 60-minute delay that could affect required app installations after Autopilot enrollment. Administrators should verify the new behavior in their environment because it can shorten device provisioning and reduce post-enrollment support issues.

articles.title_original: The Demise of the OWA Light Client
articles.headline: The Demise of the OWA Light Client
articles.summary: Microsoft will retire the lightweight OWA Light client in Exchange Server SE with an August 2026 update, requiring on-premises users to switch to the full OWA client. Available since Exchange 2007 for older browsers and alternative platforms without JavaScript, OWA Light affects only on-premises Exchange environments, not cloud-only users.

articles.title_original: ACR Stealer: Two observed intrusion chains amid increased threat activity
articles.headline: ACR Stealer: Two observed intrusion chains amid increased threat activity
articles.summary: Microsoft Defender Experts has observed increased ACR Stealer activity using ClickFix lures to trick employees into running malicious commands that steal browser passwords, session tokens, and sensitive documents, potentially enabling access to cloud resources and further intrusion. IT teams should monitor for ClickFix lures, suspicious WebDAV activity, and obfuscated PowerShell execution.

articles.title_original: Microsoft Teams: Explicit recording consent for PSTN participants now available in GCC High and DoD
articles.headline: Microsoft Teams: Explicit recording consent for PSTN participants now available in GCC High and DoD

articles.headline: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Session and Response Sharing in M365 Copilot

articles.title_original: Join Us Live: Understanding Copilot Cowork and Managing Copilot Credits
articles.headline: Microsoft schedules Copilot Cowork and Credits management AMA
articles.summary: Microsoft will host a live session on Copilot Cowork adoption, governance, scaling, and credit allocation. Administrators can use the guidance to improve usage visibility and control consumption-based AI costs.

articles.title_original: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
articles.headline: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
articles.summary: AI agents plan and chain actions across systems without human approval at every step, so overly broad role assignments can grant access far beyond what was intended. Microsoft recommends treating each agent as a separate identity with a dedicated managed identity, least-privilege RBAC, a clearly defined scope, and secure tool binding before broad deployment.

articles.headline: Renewed as Microsoft MVP for Intune and Windows 365

articles.title_original: AutoAcceptSsoPermission: The Continue to Sign In Prompt Fix
articles.headline: AutoAcceptSsoPermission: The Continue to Sign In Prompt Fix
articles.summary: The AutoAcceptSsoPermission policy closes the gap between Windows user-consent requirements and managed-device onboarding by allowing administrators to preapprove the “Continue to sign in” prompt that appears during sign-in. Rudy Ooms demonstrates how the setting works on Intune-managed devices.

articles.title_original: Admin control for SSO prompts in Windows; finally, an off switch!
articles.headline: Admin control for SSO prompts in Windows; finally, an off switch!
articles.summary: In the EEA, Windows must now ask users before reusing their sign-in for other Microsoft apps, adding noise in managed environments where SSO is already configured. Microsoft has published a new registry control that lets administrators disable the prompt, available with the July 2026 update for Windows 11 24H2 (KB5101650).

articles.title_original: How to Schedule Teams Meetings from Shared Mailboxes
articles.headline: How to Schedule Teams Meetings from Shared Mailboxes
articles.summary: Despite Microsoft documenting it as supported, uncertainty remains over whether Teams meeting invitations can be sent from a shared mailbox. It works when the delegate has full mailbox access and a Teams license, but currently only in classic Outlook, and the shared mailbox itself does not need a separate license.

articles.title_original: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
articles.headline: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
articles.summary: On July 14, Microsoft Threat Intelligence uncovered a coordinated supply-chain compromise of the @asyncapi organization on npm, with five package versions republished using the same malicious loader. Because @asyncapi/specs is a transitive dependency and the code runs at import time rather than through postinstall, affected developer machines, CI/CD pipelines, and production services are not protected by “npm install --ignore-scripts.”

Part two targets Microsoft 365 administrators and CIOs whose immature identity foundations have prevented users from accessing Copilot and left them without a control plane for future agents.

Because a compromised or manipulated agent, including one affected by prompt injection, can operate autonomously across Microsoft 365 until detected, live risk-based Conditional Access is a better option than blocking all agents.

articles.title_original: Manage Folders and Rules for Microsoft 365 Groups in Outlook
articles.headline: Manage Folders and Rules for Microsoft 365 Groups in Outlook
articles.summary: This practical guide explains how owners and members can create folders and inbox rules for Microsoft 365 group mailboxes in Outlook. Group mailboxes contain only Inbox and Deleted Items by default, so the feature can help teams organize growing email volumes, although it has limited relevance for most IT administrators.

articles.title_original: Passkeys Become the Default as Microsoft Entra Retires SMS and Voice Authentication
articles.headline: Passkeys Become the Default as Microsoft Entra Retires SMS and Voice Authentication
articles.summary: AdminDroid summarizes Microsoft’s transition to a passkey-first Entra ID model and explains why phone-based MFA poses a growing risk as organizations increase their use of AI. SMS and voice codes travel over telephone networks and are vulnerable to interception, social engineering, and SIM swapping, making the article useful background for prioritizing passkey deployment.

articles.title_original: Microsoft to Stop Providing Telephony-Based Authentication Methods for MFA in February 2027
articles.headline: Microsoft to Stop Providing Telephony-Based Authentication Methods for MFA in February 2027
articles.summary: Microsoft says in MC1426371 that it will stop providing SMS one-time codes and voice calls for MFA on February 1, 2027, and move all tenants toward passkeys as a second factor. Customers that still require SMS or voice must purchase the service from a telecommunications provider through Microsoft Security Store starting September 18, 2026, so administrators should begin deploying passkeys now to prevent users from losing their sign-in method.

articles.title_original: Defending SaaS-based applications against ShinyHunters OAuth abuse
articles.headline: Defending SaaS-based applications against ShinyHunters OAuth abuse
articles.summary: Microsoft tracked campaigns from 2025 to 2026 in which actors linked to ShinyHunters abused trusted OAuth connections, vishing, and misconfigured guest access to reach SaaS applications such as Salesforce. A single entry point can quickly lead to large-scale CRM data extraction while bypassing standard sign-in alerts, so administrators should monitor OAuth-connected apps, review consent grants, and tighten guest access.

articles.headline: The Sadly Unfulfilled Promise of the Outlook Calendar Agent
articles.summary: Tony Redmond has tested Microsoft’s Outlook Calendar Agent since its April 2026 launch but finds that it still struggles with basic meeting scheduling. A key limitation is that Microsoft 365 connectors can read but not write to user calendars, preventing genuine agentic scheduling, so IT admins should adjust expectations about current Copilot agent capabilities.

articles.headline: Announcing new builds for July 6 2026
articles.summary: Microsoft is testing Cloud Rebuild in the Experimental Channel, a recovery feature that completely reinstalls Windows 11 by downloading both the OS image and device drivers from Windows Update, even when Windows cannot start. Unlike Reset this PC, it requires neither USB media nor a separate image, simplifying recovery of locked or damaged endpoints.

articles.headline: 5 insights from Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar™ for Cloud Security Posture Management
articles.summary: Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar for Cloud Security Posture Management forecasts that the CSPM market will grow from $2.82 billion to $6.96 billion by 2030 and places Microsoft among the leaders. It highlights a shift from periodic compliance to continuous, risk-based governance within CNAPP platforms, but is primarily market and positioning material rather than an actionable change for admins.

articles.title_original: First look at the SharePoint API usage report
articles.headline: First look at the SharePoint API usage report
articles.summary: A new SharePoint API Usage report is available in Microsoft Graph beta, showing how apps use SharePoint APIs in a tenant. It is disabled by default and must be enabled through the new report settings with the ReportSettings.ReadWrite.All permission, although the endpoints do not yet enforce the documented permissions and a global admin can enable it regardless.

articles.title_original: A Deep Dive into Controlled Feature Rollout: Why Features are turned off by default
articles.headline: A Deep Dive into Controlled Feature Rollout: Why Features are turned off by default
articles.summary: Rudy Ooms examines Controlled Feature Rollout, the mechanism Microsoft uses to release new Windows features and hotfixes gradually and disabled by default. The post explains how an individual device determines when it receives a feature, helping admins understand why features appear at different times across their fleet.

articles.title_original: KB – employeeLeaveDateTime show empty (null)
articles.headline: KB – employeeLeaveDateTime show empty (null)
articles.summary: When the employeeLeaveDateTime attribute appears as null in Entra ID, the usual cause is missing permissions because this sensitive attribute requires User-LifeCycleInfo.Read.All for access through Microsoft Graph. The value becomes visible after consent is granted, which is important for admins building lifecycle or offboarding automation around departure dates.

articles.title_original: Why Windows is the hardest passkey surface in 2026 and what Entra admins should expect
articles.headline: Why Windows is the hardest passkey surface in 2026 and what Entra admins should expect
articles.summary: Data from large passkey deployments shows that Windows has the lowest sign-in success rate of any major platform in 2026, with Windows users struggling where iPhone users sign in seamlessly despite using the same identity provider and policy. Entra admins planning passkey deployments should expect their Windows fleet to generate the most support requests and pilot-test it especially thoroughly.

articles.title_original: Purview Data Loss Prevention Introduces File Quarantine
articles.headline: Purview Data Loss Prevention Introduces File Quarantine
articles.summary: Purview DLP is adding a “move to quarantine” action that isolates files violating DLP policies in a secure SharePoint location instead of merely blocking them, replacing the original with a tombstone text file that explains what happened and why. The feature is currently in preview and is scheduled for general availability in early June 2026 (MC1288527).

Team membership effectively grants broad access to sensitive content, making this useful for legal recordkeeping requirements, while membership changes can also be tracked in the audit log.

articles.title_original: Win32 App Deep Dive – What’s Inside an intunewin File and How it Gets Into Intune?
articles.headline: Win32 App Deep Dive – What’s Inside an intunewin File and How it Gets Into Intune?
articles.summary: This detailed technical guide explains how Win32 apps work in Intune, including what an .intunewin file contains, how IntuneWinAppUtil packages it, and how it is uploaded to the service. It also shows how Win32 apps can support scripting and registry changes and provide an alternative to remediations for organizations without an Enterprise license, making it a useful reference for admins deploying RMM, EDR, and other agent-based apps.

articles.title_original: Prevent Sensitive Data Leakage Across Unmanaged Apps with Purview and Entra
articles.headline: Prevent Sensitive Data Leakage Across Unmanaged Apps with Purview and Entra
articles.summary: Microsoft is launching Network Data Security, which extends Purview DLP to the network layer through Entra and detects sensitive data in motion, including text pasted into AI chatbots, files uploaded to personal cloud storage, and traffic sent to unauthorized SaaS apps beyond the reach of Microsoft 365’s built-in controls. It gives IT admins real-time visibility and the ability to block leaks that previously might only have been discovered afterward.

articles.title_original: Hands-On Microsoft 365 Pulse – Weekly Updates (Week 26)
articles.headline: Hands-On Microsoft 365 Pulse – Weekly Updates (Week 26)
articles.summary: The month’s first Pulse summarizes June updates across SharePoint, Teams, and Copilot, with the retirement of Remote Event Receivers in SharePoint Online being the most important change for admins because dependent custom solutions must now plan their transition. Custom domains can also be excluded from Copilot web grounding, while a new Recap app in Teams brings all meeting recaps together in one place.

articles.title_original: Mass convert groups from AD to Entra ID using PowerShell
articles.headline: Mass convert groups from AD to Entra ID using PowerShell
articles.summary: Entra’s new Group Source of Authority conversion allows synchronized AD groups to move to Entra ID management without losing their identity, membership, or access. Marius Solbakken provides a PowerShell script for bulk conversion with error handling and logging, saving admins from manually processing hundreds of groups during a cloud migration.

Admins relying on it for content moderation should know that it provides much weaker control than expected.

articles.title_original: Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (13th Edition) and some other news
articles.headline: Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (13th Edition) and some other news
articles.summary: Vasil Michev reports that the 2027 edition of “Microsoft 365 for IT Pros” has launched, now with two new standalone books alongside the main bundle. He also discusses Microsoft’s voluntary severance package taking effect and expresses concern about the expertise being lost as many veterans leave; for IT admins, this is primarily a reference and reading update rather than a technical change.

articles.title_original: Autopilot 0x800705b4: App Control Blocks the Intune Management Extension
articles.headline: Autopilot 0x800705b4: App Control Blocks the Intune Management Extension
articles.summary: An Autopilot provisioning process stalls at “Preparing your device for mobile management” and ends with error 0x800705b4 because an App Control policy (WDAC), rather than Autopilot itself, blocks the Intune Management Extension from running. Admins deploying App Control should explicitly allow IME or device provisioning will fail.

articles.title_original: Announcing Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (2027 Edition)
articles.headline: Announcing Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (2027 Edition)
articles.summary: Tony Redmond’s “Microsoft 365 for IT Pros” book series is now in its thirteenth edition, with the 2027 content split so that Purview and Power Platform have their own books. The 330-page Purview book covers data lifecycle management, eDiscovery, DLP, information protection, and compliance, but it is a commercial Gumroad e-book rather than Microsoft product news.

articles.title_original: ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026
articles.headline: ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026
articles.summary: Microsoft’s monthly security update introduces “MDASH” in private preview, an agent-based AI system that scans complex code to find and validate vulnerabilities and route them directly into Defender workflows, while Defender is also extending endpoint protection to local AI agents. These developments give IT admins new vulnerability tools but also reflect a growing attack surface as AI agents are adopted.

articles.title_original: Securing AI agents: When AI tools move from reading to acting
articles.headline: Securing AI agents: When AI tools move from reading to acting
articles.summary: Microsoft Incident Response describes an attack pattern in which malicious Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools manipulate AI agents that can not only read content but also perform actions such as sending email or changing systems, and provides a practical playbook for detecting, containing, and preventing these attacks with Microsoft security controls. As agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, and Azure AI Foundry gain write access, the attack surface grows and IT admins need to understand the risk.

articles.title_original: Cleanup Inactive Active Directory User Accounts Using PowerShell
articles.headline: Cleanup Inactive Active Directory User Accounts Using PowerShell
articles.summary: AdminDroid has published a PowerShell script that finds inactive Active Directory accounts and automates cleanup in a controlled workflow by disabling them, moving them to a dedicated OU, and deleting them after a defined retention period. Forgotten accounts retain unnecessary access and increase the attack surface, while the script reduces the time and errors associated with manual cleanup and accounts for lastLogonTimestamp lagging by up to 14 days.

Google removed it after notification, but organizations should consider allowlisting browser extensions because they remain an underestimated attack surface.

articles.headline: Five Eyes Intelligence Alliance Says It’s Time to Speed Up Patching
articles.summary: On June 22, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance jointly urged organizations to patch known vulnerabilities faster and phase out systems that no longer receive security updates. IT administrators should review patching schedules and end-of-life system inventories before attackers exploit the gaps.

articles.title_original: Announcing new builds for 26 June 2026, retail launch of new WIP improvements
articles.headline: Announcing new builds for 26 June 2026, retail launch of new WIP improvements
articles.summary: Microsoft is beginning to roll out the new Windows Insider setup to regular retail Windows 11 builds this week, adding new channel choices under Settings > Windows Update > Windows Insider Program. In most cases, users can now change channels or leave the program without reinstalling Windows; the rollout is gradual and accompanied by new Beta and Experimental channel builds.

articles.title_original: Hands-On Microsoft 365 Pulse – Weekly Updates (Week 25)
articles.headline: Hands-On Microsoft 365 Pulse – Weekly Updates (Week 25)
articles.summary: João Ferreira's weekly Microsoft 365 roundup focuses on Microsoft's Copilot Cowork licensing changes and the resulting reaction. Recommended articles examine the actual costs, cheaper alternatives that cover about 80% of requirements, and how licensing choices determine who can use AI at work, while the rest of the edition covers other news including SharePoint.

articles.title_original: Entra ID SAML authnmethodsreferences (AMR) now supports phishing-resistant MFA
articles.headline: Entra ID SAML authnmethodsreferences (AMR) now supports phishing-resistant MFA
articles.summary: Starting June 29, 2026, Entra ID will include both amr and acr claims in SAML 2.0 tokens, enabling applications such as Salesforce to identify the authentication method used and enforce phishing-resistant MFA through FIDO2 or passkeys for administrators. Organizations using Entra ID as the Salesforce identity provider should verify that their Entra policies apply before Salesforce enables the requirement on June 29.

articles.title_original: Intune EPM System Settings: Network and Time Sync Elevation
articles.headline: Intune EPM System Settings: Network and Time Sync Elevation
articles.summary: Microsoft appears to be developing a new system settings experience in Endpoint Privilege Management, initially covering network settings and time synchronization. It would let standard users elevate privileges for selected settings without receiving full local administrator access, giving IT more granular control over self-service tasks.

articles.title_original: What the DragonForce Attack Against Teams Is All About
articles.headline: What the DragonForce Attack Against Teams Is All About
articles.summary: DragonForce routed Backdoor.Turn malware through Microsoft Teams TURN relay servers, making command-and-control traffic resemble normal Teams activity and evade monitoring. There is no Teams vulnerability or patch; administrators should inspect traffic patterns and volumes rather than assuming all traffic to trusted Microsoft domains is safe.

articles.title_original: Photo ZIP campaign targeting hospitality industry delivers Node.js implant for persistent access
articles.headline: Photo ZIP campaign targeting hospitality industry delivers Node.js implant for persistent access
articles.summary: Since April 2026, Microsoft Threat Intelligence has tracked an ongoing multistage campaign targeting hospitality and travel organizations in Europe and Asia, using image ZIP files with fake shortcuts to launch obfuscated PowerShell, install a Node.js implant, and establish dual registry persistence with command-and-control traffic over unusual ports. The attackers use legitimate services such as Calendly and Google's URL redirection for phishing with multilingual lures, and industry administrators should hunt for these persistence mechanisms.

articles.title_original: Building reliable voice agents: A practical guide
articles.headline: Building reliable voice agents: A practical guide
articles.summary: Microsoft provides a practical guide to designing customer-facing voice agents that are reliable, not merely capable, covering patterns, testing, and operationalization from prototype to production in Copilot Studio. It is relevant to administrators considering voice-based customer service agents, where reliability determines whether automation creates value or risk.
